TRACE.Definition: follow the development or history of it

SEDIMENT.Definition: material like dirt or sand that builds up over time

DEPOSITS.Definition: layers of material left over time

CHARRED.Definition: partly burned and blackened by fire

WILDFIRE.Definition: a large uncontrolled fire in nature

UPRIGHT.Definition: standing on two legs; not bent over

PREDATOR.Definition: an animal that hunts and eats other animals

SMOULDERING.Definition: burning slowly with smoke but little flame

TENDING.Definition: looking after and taking care of it

CELLULAR.Definition: relating to the tiny parts that make up living things

STARCHES.Definition: substances in foods like potatoes, rice, and bread that give energy

HOMININS.Definition: early humans and their close ancestors

NOTICEABLY.Definition: in a way that is easy to see or notice

ANATOMICAL.Definition: relating to the structure of the body

STRIKINGLY.Definition: in a very clear and surprising way

DELAY GRATIFICATION.Definition: wait for a better reward later instead of taking a smaller reward now

CONSEQUENTIAL.Definition: very important because it had major effects

ANTHROPOLOGIST.Definition: a scientist who studied people and human societies

HUNTER-GATHERER.Definition: a person who lives by hunting animals and collecting wild plants

SPECULATED.Definition: guessed or thought about something without knowing for sure

SPECIALISATION.Definition: focusing on one particular task or skill

DIVISION OF LABOUR.Definition: sharing different jobs between different people

PROVOCATIVE.Definition: likely to make people think, argue, or react strongly

PAIR BONDING.Definition: forming a close relationship between two people

MUTUAL ADVANTAGE.Definition: a situation that helped both sides

CROUCHING.Definition: bending low close to the ground

AT THE MERCY OF.Definition: completely controlled by it and unable to change it

PERCUSSION.Definition: making sparks by hitting two hard objects together

FLINT.Definition: a hard stone that can make sparks

PYRITE.Definition: a shiny mineral that can produce sparks when struck

TINDER.Definition: dry material that catches fire easily

FRICTION.Definition: heat made when two things rub together

PEAT.Definition: partly decayed plant material used as fuel

RAN ON.Definition: used as its main source of power or energy

SMELT.Definition: heat and melt metal from rock

UNDER THEIR NOSES.Definition: very close to them and easy to find

DESPERATION.Definition: a strong feeling of needing it badly

FIRST DOMINO.Definition: the first event that caused many other events to happen

COMMODITIES.Definition: basic goods that were bought and sold

LIMITLESS.Definition: without an end or clear limit

ENERGY-DENSE.Definition: containing a large amount of energy in a small amount

REMISS.Definition: failing to say something that should be said
